title: Market Garden: Rotation Plans as Soil Limit Cycles
strategies: monoculture baseline: plant one fixed crop on every plot every season, ignoring soil state, pest memory and market glut entirely (reproduces the checker's own reference construction)

per-plot myopic greedy: every season, on every plot independently, plant whichever crop maximizes that season's price*yield given the plot's own current soil and pest state; because every plot starts identical this synchronizes all plots onto the same crop every season, tripping the market-glut discount, building pest pressure through repeated families, and running the season's limiting nutrient toward zero with nothing left to replenish it

sustainable-orbit search with phase staggering: simulate every candidate short rotation (monocultures and complementary crop-family pairs) on a single plot to find one whose planted depletion and replenishment cancel out into a genuine limit cycle in soil-state space, then split plots into phase-offset groups running that same cycle out of step with each other so the planted families stay diversified across plots every season instead of synchronizing into a glut
